context:
You are a content analyst and social media strategist. Given a YouTube video transcript, produce EXACTLY this JSON structure:
{
  "title": "original video title",
  "channel": "channel name",
  "url": "original URL",
  "date_analysed": "ISO 8601 date",
  "key_takeaways": ["takeaway 1", "takeaway 2", ...],
  "tldr": "2-3 sentence summary a busy person can read in 15 seconds",
  "twitter_hook": "social hook — see rules below",
  "topics": ["topic1", "topic2"]
}

SOCIAL HOOK RULES (strictly under 280 characters including hashtags):
- Lead with a bold claim, surprising stat, or contrarian take from the video — NOT a summary
- Use a pattern like: "[Surprising insight] — here's why it matters:" or "Most people think [X]. Actually, [Y]."
- Write in a punchy, conversational tone — as if you're telling a friend the one thing they NEED to know
- End with 2-3 relevant hashtags (count towards 280 chars)
- Do NOT start with "Just watched..." or "New video from..." — go straight to the insight
- The hook must make someone stop scrolling and want to learn more

IMPORTANT: All output must be in English. If the transcript is in another language, translate all content (key_takeaways, tldr, social hook) into fluent English.

Respond ONLY with valid JSON — no markdown fences, no preamble.
question:
Video Title: OpenAI's Quiet GPT-5 Turbo Update
Channel: Fireship
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt1demo

TRANSCRIPT:
Welcome back to the channel. Today OpenAI quietly pushed a major update to its API: structured outputs are now the default for function calling, and latency on GPT-5 Turbo dropped about 40 percent thanks to a new speculative decoding pipeline. The bigger story is pricing — input tokens are down to $1.50 per million, which puts OpenAI roughly on par with Claude Haiku for cheap workloads. Developers on Hacker News are already rewriting their agent loops to take advantage of the lower tail latency. The catch: the new model refuses long-horizon tool chains past about twelve steps, citing a new safety classifier. We tested it against Claude Sonnet 4.6 on our internal coding benchmark and saw mixed results — OpenAI wins on speed, Anthropic still leads on multi-file refactors. Hit subscribe if you want the full breakdown next week, and drop a comment with which benchmark you want us to run.
expected_response: {"title": "OpenAI's Quiet GPT-5 Turbo Update: 40% Faster, Half the Price", "channel": "Fireship", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt1demo", "date_analysed": "2026-04-17T00:00:00", "key_takeaways": ["GPT-5 Turbo latency dropped ~40% via a new speculative decoding pipeline", "Input token pricing cut to $1.50 per million, matching Claude Haiku tier", "Structured outputs are now the default for function calling in the API", "The model refuses tool chains longer than ~12 steps due to a new safety classifier", "Head-to-head: OpenAI wins on speed, Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 still leads on multi-file refactors"], "tldr": "OpenAI shipped a quiet GPT-5 Turbo update that cuts latency by 40% and halves input pricing to $1.50 per million tokens. Structured outputs are now default, but a new safety classifier blocks tool chains past twelve steps. On coding benchmarks, OpenAI wins speed while Claude Sonnet 4.6 still wins on complex refactors.", "twitter_hook": "OpenAI just halved GPT-5 Turbo input pricing to $1.50/Mtok and cut latency 40% — and almost nobody noticed. But there's a catch: it refuses tool chains past 12 steps. #AI #LLMs #DevTools", "topics": ["AI", "LLMs", "developer tools", "pricing"]}

question:
Video Title: Markets Close Lower as Fed Minutes Hit
Channel: Bloomberg Markets
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt2demo

TRANSCRIPT:
Markets closed lower today, but the real story is what happened after the bell. The Fed released the minutes from its March meeting and three governors are now publicly against any rate cut before September. Two-year yields jumped 18 basis points on the news. In equities, Nvidia was the only Mag 7 name to finish green, up 1.2 percent, after a supply-chain report showed Blackwell shipments running two weeks ahead of schedule. Energy was the worst sector, down 2.4 percent, as Brent crude slid below $68 on demand concerns out of China. The dollar index broke above 106 for the first time since January. On the credit side, high-yield spreads widened 12 basis points, the sharpest single-day move in six months. Tomorrow watch the 10-year auction at 1 pm Eastern — a weak bid-to-cover here could force a repricing of the entire belly of the curve. That's the tape.
expected_response: {"title": "Hawkish Fed Minutes Send 2-Year Yields Soaring", "channel": "Bloomberg Markets", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt2demo", "date_analysed": "2026-04-17T00:00:00", "key_takeaways": ["Three Fed governors publicly oppose any rate cut before September, per March minutes", "Two-year Treasury yields jumped 18 bps after the minutes release", "Nvidia was the only Mag 7 name to close green, up 1.2% on Blackwell shipment beat", "Energy sector down 2.4% as Brent crude fell below $68 on weak China demand", "High-yield credit spreads widened 12 bps — the sharpest daily move in six months", "DXY broke 106 for the first time since January; watch tomorrow's 10-year auction"], "tldr": "Hawkish Fed minutes pushed two-year yields up 18 bps and lifted the dollar index above 106. Equities closed lower with energy the worst sector, while Nvidia bucked the trend on a Blackwell shipment surprise. Credit stress is the signal to watch — HY spreads widened the most in six months.", "twitter_hook": "Three Fed governors just killed the September rate-cut trade. 2Y yields +18bps, DXY through 106, HY credit cracking wider than any day in 6 months. The bond vigilantes are back. #Markets #Fed #Bonds", "topics": ["markets", "Federal Reserve", "bonds", "equities", "credit"]}

question:
Video Title: Türkiye Ekonomisi Q1 Değerlendirmesi
Channel: Finans Analiz
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt3demo

TRANSCRIPT:
Merhaba arkadaşlar, bugün size Türkiye ekonomisinin son çeyrek verilerinden bahsedeceğim. Merkez Bankası faizi yüzde 45'te sabit tuttu, enflasyon ise mart ayında yıllık yüzde 68'e geriledi. Lira dolar karşısında son bir ayda yüzde üç değer kazandı ve bu durum ihracatçılar için baskı yaratıyor. Borsa İstanbul endeksi rekor kırmaya devam ediyor, bankacılık hisseleri öne çıkıyor. Yabancı yatırımcı çıkışı mart ayında durma noktasına geldi, bu çok önemli bir sinyal. Önümüzdeki hafta Hazine'nin iki tahvil ihalesi var, takip edeceğiz.

NOTE: The transcript is in language code 'tr'. Translate all output to English.

The lira firmed 3% against the dollar, squeezing exporters but attracting foreign flows. Banking-led BIST keeps setting records, and two Treasury auctions next week are the next test.", "twitter_hook": "Turkish inflation finally cracked below 70% — and foreign outflows stopped dead in March for the first time in over a year. The carry trade is quietly coming back. #EmergingMarkets #Turkey #Macro", "topics": ["Turkey", "emerging markets", "inflation", "central banks"]}

question:
Video Title: AI Labs, Defence Deals, and Chinese Export Controls
Channel: Stratechery
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt5demo

TRANSCRIPT:
Two stories converged this week and they matter more together than apart. First, Anthropic and OpenAI both announced deeper partnerships with US defence — Anthropic on classified Claude deployments inside the intelligence community, OpenAI on battlefield decision-support software with Anduril. Second, Beijing responded by tightening export controls on gallium and germanium, both critical for military-grade semiconductors. Nvidia's China revenue is already below 10 percent and this will push it lower. The bigger frame is that AI labs are no longer neutral infrastructure — they are national-security actors with picks-and-shovels providers now drawn into the same export regime. Expect more labs to follow. On the technical side, Anthropic also dropped benchmark numbers showing Claude 4.7 Opus beating every open-source model on agentic coding tasks by double digits, which is relevant because open-weight deployability was supposed to be the hedge against exactly this kind of geopolitical lock-in. If the frontier keeps pulling away from open weights, the hedge stops working. Watch for European responses — France and Germany are the two capitals that will feel squeezed first.
expected_response: {"title": "AI Labs Become National-Security Actors: Defence Deals and China's Response", "channel": "Stratechery", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yt5demo", "date_analysed": "2026-04-17T00:00:00", "key_takeaways": ["Anthropic expanded classified Claude deployments inside the US intelligence community", "OpenAI partnered with Anduril on battlefield decision-support software", "Beijing tightened gallium and germanium export controls in response", "Nvidia's China revenue share is under 10% and expected to fall further", "Claude 4.7 Opus beat every open-weights model on agentic coding by double digits", "Widening frontier-vs-open-weights gap undermines open-source as a geopolitical hedge", "France and Germany are the European capitals most exposed to the squeeze"], "tldr": "AI labs are no longer neutral infrastructure — Anthropic and OpenAI are both deepening US defence partnerships while Beijing retaliates with critical-mineral export controls. At the same time, Claude 4.7 Opus is pulling further ahead of open-weights models, eroding the one hedge buyers had against geopolitical lock-in. Europe is next to feel the pressure.", "twitter_hook": "AI labs just stopped being neutral infrastructure. Anthropic is inside US intel, OpenAI is building targeting software with Anduril, and Beijing is choking off gallium. The open-weights hedge is failing at the worst possible moment. #AI #Geopolitics #Semiconductors", "topics": ["AI", "geopolitics", "semiconductors", "national security", "open source"]}

    {
      "author_username": "username",
      "text": "post text",
      "url": "post url",
      "why_notable": "reason this post stands out"
    }
  ],
  "post_count": 42
}

ANALYSIS RULES:
- Synthesise across ALL accounts — find common themes and disagreements
- Focus on the provided topics — ignore off-topic posts
- key_takeaways: 4-8 items that capture the most important insights
- notable_posts: 3-5 posts that are particularly noteworthy, with why_notable explaining the reason
- post_count: total number of posts analysed

SOCIAL HOOK RULES (strictly under 280 characters including hashtags):

- Use a pattern like: "[Surprising insight] — here's why it matters:" or "Most people think [X]. Actually, [Y]."

- The hook must make someone stop scrolling and want to learn more

IMPORTANT: All output must be in English.

Respond ONLY with valid JSON — no markdown fences, no preamble.

Topics: AI
Total posts: 3

@karpathy (2026-04-16T14:22:00Z) [likes:842 rt:98]
The most underrated AI skill in 2026 is writing evals. Everyone has the same models now; the moat is knowing if your output is actually good.

Cost per useful task from GPT-5 is down 100x in 18 months. The next 100x is in agent loops, not model parameters.

You are a content analyst and social media strategist. Given a collection of posts from X (Twitter), produce EXACTLY this JSON structure:
{
  "title": "descriptive digest title",
  "accounts": ["username1", "username2"],
  "topics": ["topic1", "topic2"],
  "key_takeaways": ["takeaway 1", "takeaway 2", ...],
  "tldr": "2-3 sentence summary a busy person can read in 15 seconds",
  "twitter_hook": "social hook — see rules below",
  "notable_posts": [
    {
      "author_username": "username",
      "text": "post text",
      "url": "post url",
      "why_notable": "reason this post stands out"
    }
  ],
  "post_count": 42
}

ANALYSIS RULES:
- Synthesise across ALL accounts — find common themes and disagreements
- Focus on the provided topics — ignore off-topic posts
- key_takeaways: 4-8 items that capture the most important insights
- notable_posts: 3-5 posts that are particularly noteworthy, with why_notable explaining the reason
- post_count: total number of posts analysed

SOCIAL HOOK RULES (strictly under 280 characters including hashtags):
- Lead with a bold claim, surprising stat, or contrarian take from the posts — NOT a summary
- Use a pattern like: "[Surprising insight] — here's why it matters:" or "Most people think [X]. Actually, [Y]."
- Write in a punchy, conversational tone — as if you're telling a friend the one thing they NEED to know
- End with 2-3 relevant hashtags (count towards 280 chars)
- Do NOT start with "Just read..." or "Today on X..." — go straight to the insight
- The hook must make someone stop scrolling and want to learn more

IMPORTANT: All output must be in English.

Respond ONLY with valid JSON — no markdown fences, no preamble.
question:

Topics: AI

@ylecun (2026-04-16T09:00:00Z) [likes:4100 rt:520]
LLMs are a dead end for AGI. We need world models. Scaling autoregressive text is not going to get you to human-level intelligence, no matter how much compute you burn.
URL: https://x.com/ylecun/status/20

@sama (2026-04-16T10:00:00Z) [likes:8900 rt:1120]
Every year someone writes the "LLMs have hit a wall" post. Every year the models get dramatically better. This year will be no different.
URL: https://x.com/sama/status/21

@ylecun (2026-04-16T11:00:00Z) [likes:2800 rt:340]
Benchmark improvements are not reasoning improvements. Goodhart's law is eating the AGI discourse.
URL: https://x.com/ylecun/status/22

@sama (2026-04-16T12:00:00Z) [likes:6700 rt:810]
GPT-5's internal eval on novel math is now above the median Anthropic researcher. Keep telling yourself we've plateaued.
URL: https://x.com/sama/status/23
